facts.context: A wetland was drained to build a mall. Two years later, there were no more toads in that area. Why did the toads disappear?
facts.truth: The toads got their food from the wetland ecosystem.
distractors: The toads were destroyed by the construction equipment.
The toads died because toads cannot breathe out of water.
The toads were frightened and went into the woods.

Which are replenished more quickly than they are used?

facts.context: Which of these tools is best for recording observations outdoors?
Students are investigating the physical properties of three solid mixtures by placing each solid mixture in liquid water. What physical property are the students testing?
What remains in the same location in the sky of the Northern Hemisphere each night?

A student performs an investigation using anti-bacterial hand soap. The student washes only his left hand with anti-bacterial soap, but cultures swabs from both hands. The cultures from both hands were incubated at room temperature for 48 hours before being examined. Which best explains the purpose of using soap on only the left hand?
facts.truth: to serve as the independent variable for the investigation
distractors: to act as a controlled variable for the investigation
to act as the dependent variable for the investigation
to provide another testable variable for the investigation

facts.context: Raquel fed a litter of five kittens an all-meat diet for two weeks and found that they gained an average of 15 ounces. She concluded that eating meat is the best way to help kittens grow. What should Raquel have done to make this a better experiment?
facts.truth: tested five kittens on a non-meat diet also
distractors: tested dogs as well as cats

facts.context: In the 1600s a scientist named Anton van Leeuwenhoek worked toward improving microscopes and created his own lenses. With his microscopes, Leeuwenhoek discovered bacteria and muscle fibers. Which type of job benefited most from these discoveries?
One advantage of a hand lens over a microscope is that the hand lens
facts.truth: is easier to carry on field investigations.
distractors: is less likely to be infected by bacteria.
makes very tiny objects appear larger.

facts.context: Raising livestock, such as sheep and cattle, results in the production of large amounts of methane gas (CH4). Methane has the same effect in the atmosphere as carbon dioxide (CO2). As more livestock are raised to provide food for humans, what is the most likely effect of the increase in methane?
Which is most directly responsible for building up a river delta?

facts.truth: It melts to form liquid water.
distractors: It condenses to form liquid water.
It freezes to become heavier ice.
It evaporates to become a vapor (gas).

facts.context: Which situation is the best example of an inherited trait?
distractors: a boy playing with a ball

facts.context: Which statement describes the reason why an ice cube melts when set out in a warm room?
facts.truth: Heat from the room is absorbed by the ice cube.
distractors: The ice cube molecules move more slowly.
One side of the ice cube is colder than the other.
Heat from the ice cube moves into the air.

facts.context: Which best explains why population growth can result in water shortages and rationing in certain areas?
distractors: Lake water is reserved for recreation.
Tall buildings impede the development of rain clouds.

facts.context: Professor Walden studies genetics at a local university. She believes that she has discovered information that contradicts a widely accepted theory. As a scientist and leader in the study of genetics, which action should she take?
facts.truth: Challenge the old theory with scientific evidence.
distractors: Announce to the world that her theory is better than the old theory.
Call the news media and tell them about her new theory.
Request that the old theory be banned.

facts.context: Canadian physician Sir William Osler once said, "In Science the credit goes to the man who convinces the world, not to the man to whom the idea first occurred." How does this quote apply to scientific knowledge?
facts.truth: Ideas must be communicated to the public
distractors: Famous scientists have the best ideas.
In order to be important, ideas must be believable.
The results are less important than the ideas in an experiment.

facts.context: Which event or principle cannot have its validity directly tested by laboratory experiment?
distractors: the Acceleration of Objects due to Gravity

facts.context: How do the circulatory system and the respiratory system depend on one another?
facts.truth: Oxygen collected by the respiratory system is carried throughout the body by the circulatory system.
distractors: Solid wastes collected by the circulatory system are carried throughout the body by the respiratory system.
Nutrients collected by the respiratory system are carried throughout the body by the circulatory system.
Carbon dioxide collected by the circulatory system is carried throughout the body by the respiratory system.

facts.context: Which factor has the greatest effect on a community's stores of freshwater?
distractors: the location of the nearest ocean
the location of the nearest mountain

facts.truth: one red straw and one green straw.
distractors: either one red straw or one green straw.

facts.truth: a star composed of hydrogen and helium.
distractors: a medium planet with an atmosphere.
a star composed of carbon and nitrogen.

facts.context: Which of the following is a characteristic of the "steady state theory" of the universe?
facts.truth: The nature of the universe has always been the same.
distractors: The universe is constantly growing and changing.

facts.context: A dietary supplement manufacturer developed a new calcium supplement that appears to increase the bone density in middle-aged women. facts.truth: test the supplement on a larger sample of women

Which of the following statements best describes the process of natural selection in a population?

distractors: All individuals develop traits to match changes to their environment.
All individuals learn to adapt to specific changes that occur slowly in their environment.
Some individuals respond to their environment by mutating to make themselves more likely to survive.

facts.truth: to restore the area to a natural condition.
distractors: to prevent wind from weathering and eroding surface coal.
